item.title: Review of DDA laws timely
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Constitutional Law Reform Commission (CLRC) will review the laws on DDA

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts: Independence statement from the President of Bougainville
item.description: [Bougainville News] My fellow Bougainvilleans, In the next few days, the resolve of the 97.8 percent of Bougainvilleans who voted for Bougainville to attain independence will be tested by the tabling of the Bi-Partisan Report on the 2019 Bougainville Referendum in the … Continue reading →

item.title: Eight tips for creating a culture of innovation in Papua New Guinea
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Peter Williams has been advising businesses on innovation for a long time as part of his role as Chief Edge Officer at Deloitte’s Centre for the Edge in Australia. He shared his top tips for creating a successful culture of innovation at the recent 2026 Innovation PNG Awards in Port Moresby. The pos

item.title: USP climbs more than 400 places in Times Higher Education Sustainability Impact Rankings and secures Top 10 World University Innovation category ranking
item.description: [Education News PNG] The University of the South Pacific (USP) has strengthened its standing among the world’s internationally recognized universities after climbing more than 400 places in the The post USP climbs more than 400 places in Times Higher Education Sustainability Impact Rankings and secures Top 10 World Univ

item.title: PNG Restricts Australian Poultry Imports After Bird Flu Discovery
item.description: [Inside PNG] NAQIA restricts Australian poultry imports after H5N1 bird flu found in WA wild birds. Free-range WA products suspended whilst barn-raised are safe. The post PNG Restricts Australian Poultry Imports After Bird Flu Discovery first appeared on Inside PNG . The post PNG Restricts Australian Poultry Imp

item.title: PNG Government backs review to protect SME funding integrity
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] PNG Government backs review to protect SME funding integrity The PNG Government has confirmed a review of its flagship SME Support Programme following concerns raised over the administration of K200 million in funding designed to support small business development nationwide. The initiative is part

item.title: Women in PNG Politics
item.description: [Pawa TV] Papua New Guinea's political history highlights persistent gender inequality, with women facing challenges in attaining parliamentary representation, despite various reform efforts and initiatives aimed at increasing their visibility and participation. The post Women in PNG Politics first appeared o

item.title: The Coolest Vanity Apps for You and Your Girls

These were the questions that the multilingual friends Katy and Sara posed themselves when they determined to learn English in one week, to prove that it can be done and any

item.title: PNG Government orders review into SME funding programme amid concerns
item.description: [PNG Facts] The PNG Government has moved to tighten oversight of its Small and Medium Enterprise (SME) Support Programme following growing concerns over how funds intended for local entrepreneurs have been administered. The review is aimed at restoring confidence in a programme central to the country’s economic

item.title: Final cohort completes health executive leadership program
item.description: [Post Courier] Australia is working with the Asian Development Bank (ADB) to support the next generation of Papua New Guinean health sector leaders. item.title: Teamwork Attracted Key Partners to Transform a ward in Markham
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By FREDRICK JEJERI A COMBINATION of teamwork has attracted key stakeholders to invest in transforming a ward area in Umi Local Level Government council in Markham District, Morobe. A combination of teamwork from parents, general members of communities and mandated politicians has gained confidence a

item.title: St John staff member Jethro Philemon honored for decade of service
item.description: [The PNG Sun] National St John Ambulance PNG recognized Jethro Philemon for ten years of service during the organization’s 69th anniversary celebrations last week on St John Day in Port Moresby. He was honored for a career marked by leadership, sacrifice and dedication to emergency services. Mr. Philemon, who cur

item.title: Mt. Bagana na El Niño givim hevi long Bogenvil
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Maunten paia long Mt. Bagana wantaim drai bilong El Nino i kamapim planti hevi long ol manmeri long Bogenvil. Ol i sot long gutpela wara na kaikai long ples we i stap klostu long we asis bilong maunten i pundaun.

item.title: PNG and the Failure of EUDR Benchmarking: When “Low Risk” Ignores Reality
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Papua New Guinea’s designation as “low risk” in the EUDR may now be one of the clearest examples of the disconnect with reality.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts :Bougainville Landowners demand mining companies halt reopening of copper pit
item.description: [Bougainville News] Traditional landowners are fighting back against plans to reopen an existing Bougainville copper mine which has remained dormant for nearly four decades. During a public event on Bougainville Island last week a warning was declared against the companies who landowners … Continue reading →

item.title: Waste not, feed more: poultry farm’s award-winning solution has wings
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] A small poultry farm in Papua New Guinea’s Central Province has found a sustainable way of reducing its dependence on expensive stock feed. Its solution, which harnesses the power of a humble insect, was one of the big winners in this year’s Innovation PNG Awards. The post Waste not, feed more: poul

item.title: Ten (10) Arrested Including Two (2) Chinese Nationals Over Alleged Murder Of Young PNG Man
item.description: [Inside PNG] Police in Port Moresby have arrested ten suspects over the death of a 20 year old man and the attempted murder of another victim. Among the arrests were four security guards from Fix Security Firm, four shop assistants and two Chinese expatriates from Cash and Carry Shopping Center at Gerehu’s Rainb

item.title: PNG Health Access Strengthened Through Australia Aviation Partnership
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] PNG Health Access Strengthened Through Australia Aviation Partnership A new partnership between Australia and Adventist Aviation Services is set to improve the delivery of health services and essential supplies to remote communities throughout Papua New Guinea. The agreement will bolster the operati

item.title: Tourism in PNG Compared to Fiji
item.description: [Pawa TV] Fiji significantly leads in tourism development over Papua New Guinea, generating USD 2.5 billion compared to PNG's USD 182.94 million, highlighting the need for PNG to improve accessibility and marketing. The post Tourism in PNG Compared to Fiji first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.title: PNG Commerce Minister Denies Misuse Claims, Calls Social Media Posts Defamatory
item.description: [PNG Facts] Commerce and Industry Minister Win Bakri Daki Papua New Guinea's Commerce and Industry Minister Win Bakri Daki has strongly rejected allegations circulating on social media accusing him of misappropriating public funds, describing the posts as a deliberate attempt to damage his public standing ahead

item.title: Pilot tract road widening, upgrading and gravelling in Wabag District
item.description: [Post Courier] The Yalu pilot tract road project, connecting more than a dozen villages in the Upper Aiyel Valley of the Laiap local level government area within Wabag District, is being widened, with upgrading and gravelling carried out by the Wabag District Development Authority. item.title: CIMC Joins UNDP Citizen’s Budget Workshop
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] THE Consultative Implementation and Monitoring Council (CIMC) recently joined civil society organizations (CSOs) and advocacy bodies for an intensive four-day workshop focused on Social Accountability and raising awareness on the importance of a Citizen’s Budget in Papua New Guinea. A Citizen’s Budg

item.title: Hula marks 150 years since arrival of Christian Mission in Papua New Guinea
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The people of Hula in Central Province on the 23rd of June, commemorated 150 years since the arrival of missionary Reverend Dr William George Lawes in Papua New Guinea. The event was held at Hula village where the first arrival took place. The event marked an event widely recognized as the beginning

item.title: Pisin mekim na balus bungim hevi long Goroka
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Ol pisin i bin pasim ranwe long Goroka ples balus na i givim hevi long wanpela balus bilong PNG Air. Tupela wil bilong balus i bruk tasol no gat manmeri i kisim bagarap long dispela hevi.

item.title: Sukundimi Walks Before Me nominated for awards
item.description: [Act Now PNG] The striking documentary 'Sukundimi Walks Before Me' has been nominated for both the Documentary Australia Award for Best Documentary and the Sustainable Future Award

item.title: Bougainville News Alert : Statement from the Office of the President: Response to Chief John L Momis, GCL, former President of Bougainville
item.description: [Bougainville News] It is disappointing to see a leader of John Momis’ stature, a former two term President of Bougainville and longtime Regional Member for Bougainville trying to undermine the good work of the Autonomous Bougainville Government. item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Dr Ben Hamer, an international expert on the future of work, shares his views on which skills will be most in demand in the economies of the future – and where Papua New Guinea fits into the changing nature of work. With fresh leadership now in place, the state-owned air navigation service provide

item.title: Early Campaigning in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Concerns grow in Papua New Guinea over early campaigning, which breaches electoral laws. Enforcement is weak, with limited consequences for offenders, raising issues about electoral integrity and public trust. The post Early Campaigning in PNG first appeared on PAWA TV .

item.description: [PNG Facts] Australia has entered a new partnership with Adventist Aviation Services (AAS) to expand access to lifesaving health services for people living in some of Papua New Guinea's most isolated communities. The initiative will strengthen air transport services that provide emergency medical evacuations, d

item.title: Parkop Calls for Stronger Voice for Women
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By ESWIN PLESIO THE National Capital District (NCD) Women’s Council held its first Annual General Meeting (AGM) in 14 years yesterday, bringing together more than 4,000 women at Sir John Guise Stadium in a renewed push to strengthen women’s leadership and representation. item.description: [Act Now PNG] Government plan to establish four regional centres for timber processing will fail unless it first puts an end to widespread illegal logging

item.description: [Bougainville News] FIVE YEARS ago we farewelled GC Sir Michael Somare with the most deserving tributes around the country. We also paused to remember him as someone whose political life is synonymous with the birth of PNG as a nation state … Continue reading →

item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] SP Brewery’s beers have been sold in PNG since 1952, almost a quarter-century before independence. item.title: Cancer Treatment in PNG
item.description: [Pawa TV] Cancer poses a significant health challenge in Papua New Guinea, exacerbated by late diagnoses, limited screening, and inadequate treatment facilities. Accompanied by a Fox League television crew, Johns' visit highlights the growing i

item.description: [The PNG Sun] Public servants planning to contest the 2027 National General Election have been advised to resign from their positions no later than 29 October 2026. The deadline is contained in a new Circular Instruction issued by the Department of Personnel Management (DPM), reminding intending candidates to com

item.title: Graun bruk katim Sepik Haiwe
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] Bikpela ren i mekim graun i bruk na bagarapim rot long Sepik Haiwe. Dispela rot i save bungim Is na Wes Sepik na i go olgeta long boda bilong PNG wantaim Indonesia.

item.description: [Bougainville News] The first permanent German presence in Kieta, Bougainville, began with the establishment of a Marist mission in 1901, followed by an official German colonial administrative office in 1905 to oversee the region. item.title: PNG Ready to Share Mining Experience as Solomon Islands Plans Resource Growth

item.title: The Story of the Meri Blouse
item.description: [Pawa TV] A Symbol of PNG Identity. The meri blouse, locally also called kolos in Tok Pisin, is one of the most recognisable garments associated with women in Papua New Guinea (PNG). Featuring a loose, comfortable fit often crafted from colourful fabric with bold motifs, it serves not just as clothing but as

item.title: Gereka road upgrade links communities
item.description: [Post Courier] The National Capital District Commission (NCDC) is undertaking the complete reconstruction, rehabilitation and resealing of the road segment connecting Gereka to the Reuben Taureka Highway, a major infrastructure upgrade set to improve access and mobility for more than 12,000 residents and strengthe

item.title: BSP Commits K50,000 for New Multipurpose Hall
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By GEORGINA MICHAEL BANK South Pacific (BSP) Financial Group Limited has committed K50,000 to Tubusereia Junior High School for a new multipurpose hall under its BSP 50 for 50 ‘Givim Back’ initiative. The commitment was announced at a ground-breaking ceremony attended by BSP Chief Executive Officer

item.description: [Post Courier] After months of anticipation, weather authorities have officially declared that an El Niño event is active in the Pacific. The post The weather bureau has just declared an El Niño appeared first on Post Courier .

item.title: Opposition calls for supplementary budget on El Niño
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By DALCY LULUA THE Opposition has called on the government to table a supplementary budget during Parliament’s July sitting, warning that urgent funding is needed to prepare the country for the impacts of the looming El Niño. item.description: [Act Now PNG] Communities in remote Nakanai region of West New Britain say their forests are being illegally destroyed by a Malaysian logging company

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts :Rio Tinto urged to accelerate action on remediation of Panguna mine disaster, one year on from investigation
item.description: [Bougainville News] One year on from the release of an independent investigation into Rio Tinto’s former Panguna mine in Bougainville, communities living with the ongoing environmental impact are calling on the company to urgently move towards funding solutions, particularly in areas identified … Continue reading →

item.title: ‘We’re challenging ourselves and our competitors to do better’: Kina Bank’s CEO on the push for innovation in PNG’s finance sector
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Kina Bank has been leading the way in bringing digital banking products to Papua New Guineans, most recently with the launch of the Pei Beta digital wallet earlier this year. Its CEO Ivan Vidovich shared his bank’s vision on digital payments and innovation in an on-stage interview at the Innovation

item.title: USP Entrepreneurial Fair 2026 brings powerful ideas and youthful energy to Laucala campus
item.description: [Education News PNG] The University of the South Pacific (USP) has launched its 2026 Entrepreneurial Fair, bringing together students, academics, and industry partners under the theme “AI, Innovation and The post USP Entrepreneurial Fair 2026 brings powerful ideas and youthful energy to Laucala campus first appeared on

item.title: PNG Faces High Likelihood of Extraordinary El Niño and Prolonged Drought
item.description: [Inside PNG] PNG braces for an extraordinary dry season with looming water shortages and crop stress. Districts ordered to map local water sources. The post PNG Faces High Likelihood of Extraordinary El Niño and Prolonged Drought first appeared on Inside PNG . The post PNG Faces High Likelihood of Extraordinary

item.title: PNG Receives First Shipment of HIV Self-Test Kits for National Distribution
item.description: [One Papua New Guinea] PNG health authorities have received the first shipment of HIV self-testing kits as part of a nationwide campaign designed to improve testing coverage and strengthen efforts to combat the spread of HIV. The arrival of 26,000 self-screening kits marks the beginning of a broader distribution program t

item.title: The Origins of Tok Pisin
item.description: [Pawa TV] Tok Pisin is one of the main languages spoken in Papua New Guinea (PNG) and plays a vital role in communication across the country. With over 800 languages spoken nationwide, Tok Pisin serves as a unifying language that allows people from different regions to understand each other. It is an English-

item.title: Different New Year Celebrations Around the World
item.description: [PNG Buzz] What’s possible in a week? If you dedicated seven days to the achievement of one goal, how ambitious could you make this goal? These were the questions that the multilingual friends Katy and Sara posed themselves when they determined to learn English in one week, to prove that it can be done and any

item.title: PNG Offers Mining Expertise to Help Solomon Islands Build Stronger Resource Sector
item.description: [PNG Facts] Papua New Guinea has offered to help Solomon Islands shape its mining future by sharing decades of experience in resource development while encouraging PNG-owned mining companies to explore investment opportunities across the neighbouring nation. The proposal was raised during talks in Port Moresby

item.title: NCDPHA commends IPC training collaboration with Brian Bell Chemical Division
item.description: [Post Courier] The National Capital District Provincial Health Authority (NCDPHA) has acknowledged the Infection Prevention and Control (IPC) training conducted at Gerehu General Hospital, facilitated by Brian Bell Chemical Division representatives Donwell Kodzwa and Jane Morea. To access this post, you must purch

item.title: CRCEG secures over K1 Billion in West Sepik infrastructure contracts
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] CHINA Railway Construction Engineering Group (PNG) Limited (CRCEG) has been awarded two multi-million-kina contracts for West Sepik Province valued at more than K1 billion. The first contract is for the design and construction of Sepik Highway from Warasikau Bridge to Lumi-Karaitem Road – 89.35km- u

item.title: Hoskins Airport Upgrade reaches 57 per cent as new operational restrictions take effect
item.description: [The PNG Sun] Airline operators and passengers using Hoskins Airport will face temporary operational restrictions as runway upgrade works enter the next construction phase, according to the National Airports Corporation (NAC). In a statement, NAC confirmed that the project has now reached 57 per cent completion.

item.title: La Nina bai kam - gavman mas redi
item.description: [Wantok Niuspepa] National Weather Service (NWS) bilong PNG i tok 'La Nina' bai kam klostu nau tasol i no gat wanpela toksave i kam long gavman yet long wanem samting ol bai mekim o sapos ol i redi long wanem kain ol hevi dispela taim nogut bai nap long bringim.

item.title: Laundering cases in PNG enforce historic round log export ban
item.description: [Act Now PNG] An international financial crackdown is underway in PNG with global intelligence agency INTERPOL

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : OBEC returns 45 writs, marks completion of 2025 ABG General Election
item.description: [Bougainville News] Bougainville Electoral Commissioner, Desmond Tsianai, today formally returned 45 writs comprising the Presidency, 38 single-member constituencies and the six reserved regional seats for women and Former Combatants. Tsianai formally handed the election writs to the outgoing Speaker, Simon Pentanu, of

item.title: People Moves: Nambawan Super, PNG CORE, Pacific Lime & Cement
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] Executive appointments at Pacific Lime & Cement and Nambawan Super, leadership appointment at PNG CORE. The post People Moves: Nambawan Super, PNG CORE, Pacific Lime & Cement appeared first on Business Advantage PNG .

item.title: Breadfruit is a Food Often Taken for Granted
item.description: [Inside PNG] NARI urges PNG to prioritize breadfruit as a key food security crop to protect local communities from severe El Niño droughts. The post Breadfruit is a Food Often Taken for Granted first appeared on Inside PNG . The post Breadfruit is a Food Often Taken for Granted appeared first on Inside PNG .

item.title: PNG Moves Closer to Rice Self-Sufficiency with Korean Farm Equipment Boost
item.description: [PNG Facts] PNG Moves Closer to Rice Self-Sufficiency with Korean Farm Equipment Boost/Photo supplied Papua New Guinea's drive to cut rice imports has received fresh momentum with the arrival of modern agricultural machinery for the Norikori Rice Project, reinforcing efforts to increase domestic food production

item.title: Why the world’s heatwaves are a global health emergency
item.description: [Post Courier] Heat is no longer a future climate risk. It is already here. The post Why the world’s heatwaves are a global health emergency appeared first on Post Courier .

item.title: U.S and PNG join forces to disrupt criminal networks
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] THE Letter of Agreement on law enforcement cooperation between United States and Papua New Guinea has positioned the countries to effectively work together to disrupt criminal networks, protect vulnerable populations, and strengthen governance. Through this strategic partnership dialogue, the U.S. D

item.title: PNG offers Mining Policy Support to Solomon Islands, welcomes investment opportunities
item.description: [The PNG Sun] PNG Prime Minister James Marape has offered his country’s mining policy and resource governance expertise to support Solomon Islands’ resource development while welcoming opportunities for PNG mining companies to invest in future projects. During bilateral talks in Port Moresby with Solomon Islands

item.title: Call on Forest Minister and Board to explain failure to cancel FCA permits
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW! is calls on the Forest Minster and National Forest Board to explain why controversial FCA logging permits have not been cancelled.

item.title: Bougainville News Alerts : Could tourism lead Bougainville into the future ?
item.description: [Bougainville News] Bougainville has gone through many turbulent periods over the past 100 years, not least the Civil War of the 1990s, which claimed as many as 20,000 lives. The government is now looking to develop the economy and has its eye … Continue reading →

item.title: High tech and low tech: Innovation PNG 2026 Awards winners announced
item.description: [Business Advantage PNG] The winners of Papua New Guinea’s major awards for innovation were announced at a special breakfast on 3 June at the Hilton Hotel in Port Moresby. While two of the awards were built on PNG's growing digital backbone, the third award involved a decidedly offline solution. The post High tech and low t

item.title: PNG Customs Busts Gold-Smuggling Operation
item.description: [Inside PNG] Nine Chinese nationals arrested in PNG's biggest gold-smuggling bust. 14.5kg of gold sand found on flight PX8. Strict bail granted. The post PNG Customs Busts Gold-Smuggling Operation first appeared on Inside PNG . The post PNG Customs Busts Gold-Smuggling Operation appeared first on Inside PNG .

item.title: Solomon Airlines Opens New Air Link Between Papua New Guinea and Solomon Islands
item.description: [PNG Facts] A new chapter in regional aviation will begin next month as Solomon Airlines launches scheduled flights linking Papua New Guinea and the Solomon Islands, providing the first direct service between Port Moresby and Honiara under the carrier's latest network expansion. The route, which starts on July

item.title: CEFI and The Salvation Army strengthen Financial Inclusion
item.description: [Post Courier] The Centre for Excellence in Financial Inclusion (CEFI), in partnership with The Salvation Army has successfully concluded a five-day Financial Literacy Training of Trainers (ToT) program. To access this post, you must purchase Web , Web – One-Day , Web – 14-Day , Web – One-Month , Web – Three-Month

item.title: Young fighter steals the show
item.description: [The PNG Bulletin] By GABRIEL KATUHO Samson Joe from Mendi, Southern Highlands province stole the show yesterday under 70kg division after knocking down his opponent several times in all three rounds during the Last Man Standing tournament at the Taurama Aquatics Center in Port Moresby. During the media interview yest

item.title: NGCB donates K300, 000 to PNG Kickboxing Federation
item.description: [The PNG Sun] The National Gaming Control Board (NGCB) has contributed K300,000 to the PNG Kickboxing Federation to deliver its Last Man Standing Series. The series is a youth-focused sporting initiative aimed at promoting discipline, healthy lifestyles and athlete development nationwide. Related news: https://ww

item.title: Call on Forest Minister and Board to explain failure to cancel FCA permits
item.description: [Act Now PNG] ACT NOW is calling on the Forest Minster and National Forest Board to explain why controversial FCA logging permits have not been cancelled

item.title: PNG Capital Port Moresby Faces Extended Power Pressure as Sirinumu Dam Levels Fall Under El Niño Impact
item.description: [PNG Facts] Residents of Port Moresby are being warned to expect continued pressure on electricity supply as PNG Power Ltd scales back water usage at Sirinumu Dam due to worsening drought conditions. The decision comes as rainfall levels remain far below normal across Central Province. PNG Capital Port Moresby

item.title: K8 billion lost in illicit forestry activities under investigation
item.description: [Act Now PNG] Estimated K8.78 billion lost through illicit forestry activities and suspected money laundering schemes is now at the centre of four major investigations under Operation Arcadia, a multi-agency crackdown supported by INTERPOL.

item.title: PNG signs fresh bilateral cooperation framework with Solomon Islands
item.description: [PNG Facts] A new agreement designed to expand economic and development cooperation between Papua New Guinea and the Solomon Islands has been signed in Port Moresby, setting out a roadmap for collaboration from 2026 through to 2030. The deal followed formal bilateral meetings between Prime Minister James Marape
